page: Microsoft Azure F16ams_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Famsv7 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU features 48 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 with VNNI and BF16 support, alongside cryptography extensions such as AES and SHA_NI. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, but it is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Benchmark results show top-tier single-core performance. In stress-ng single-core CPU tests, the instance ranks in the top 10%, a result mirrored in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression tests. Multi-threaded performance is also strong, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression scoring in the top 25%, while stress-ng multi-core CPU performance falls in the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth benchmarks, including cached and uncached read/write operations, consistently place in the middle 50% tier. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the server demonstrates top-tier text generation speeds of 446.22 tokens per second using a small 135M parameter model, though performance scales down to average tiers for larger 7B and 70B models.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation, high clock speed, and 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the F16ams_v7 offers a balanced profile for memory-intensive workloads that require strong single-thread execution. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs represents a tradeoff, making this instance most cost-efficient for workloads that rely on network-attached Premium Storage rather than high-speed local NVMe. It is suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, enterprise web serving, and lightweight LLM inference tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure F16ams_v7 is an x86_64 server instance from the Famsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 16-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and SHA_NI. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong multi-threaded decompression capabilities. Memory bandwidth and multi-core CPU performance sit in the average tier. This memory-intensive instance is suited for database workloads, enterprise web serving, and lightweight language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F16ams_v7 is a memory-intensive server featuring a 16-core AMD EPYC processor at 4.5 GHz, 128 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core CPU and compression benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F16ams_v7 is a memory-intensive server with a 16-core AMD EPYC CPU, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring dedicated high-frequency AMD EPYC cores, advanced vector extensions, and top-tier single-threaded performance.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 cores running at 4.5 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per core)
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Supports AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and cryptographic hardware acceleration
No local storage or GPU resources included
Suited for databases, memory-intensive applications, and web serving
